system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)

    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 8 9 6 3 11 10 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Lower part of bend
    hex (11 12 13 10 3 15 14 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 16 17 13 15 19 18 14) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 4 5 1)
            (3 7 6 2)
            (0 4 7 3)
            (1 5 6 2)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 8 9 6)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (8 11 12 9)

            (11 12 15 10)
            (3 15 14 2)

            // Block 2 walls
            (12 16 17 13)
            (15 19 18 14)
            (12 16 19 15)
            (13 17 18 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timePrecision   6;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block is "inside-out"

The primary error is:
```
Block hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) ... is inside-out
```

This means the vertex ordering in the hex block definition does not follow the right-hand rule required by OpenFOAM for valid hexahedral cells. Looking at the current `blockMeshDict`, there are several problems:

### Problems with the Current Block Definitions

1. **Block 0**: `hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2)` — The vertex ordering is incorrect. For a hex block, the first four vertices define one face and the last four define the opposite face, with consistent winding.

2. **Blocks 1a and 2**: These blocks reference vertices that don't exist in the defined vertex list (vertices 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 are defined but then blocks try to access combinations that create non-convex or inverted hexes).

3. **Missing patches**: The boundary definitions reference patches like `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` that don't exist in the `blockMeshDict`. Instead, the boundary uses generic labels like `inlet`, `outlet`, and `walls`.

### Recommended Solution

**Redefine the block topology correctly**, following the pattern from the squareBend tutorial exactly. The approach should be:

1. **Define only 4 vertices per block** (not more than 8 per block definition), ensuring proper right-hand-rule ordering.
2. **Fix Block 0**: Change to something like `hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` depending on the desired geometry orientation.
3. **Simplify the mesh design**: Given the complexity introduced by the current multi-block approach with many vertices and blocks, consider simplifying to a single straight duct with a bend represented through a different method (e.g., using `arc` edges or a simpler block decomposition).
4. **Remove unused vertices**: Vertices 12-19 may not be needed if the outlet section is just an extension of the bend arm.
5. **Ensure all boundary patches referenced in BC files match those in `blockMeshDict`**. Since the `.0/` files reference `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall`, these should either be added to `blockMeshDict` or the boundary conditions should be updated to match existing patch names (`walls`).

Alternatively, and preferably, **rewrite the entire `blockMeshDict`** based on the actual vertex layout from the squareBend tutorial, ensuring each hex block has properly ordered vertices that form valid convex hexahedra.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error — since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated, so `rhoSimpleFoam` cannot find the mesh files. This will resolve automatically once `blockMesh` succeeds.

---

## Additional Issue: Boundary Condition Patch Mismatches

Many of the `0/` field files (T, U, p) reference patches like `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` that don't exist in the current `blockMeshDict`. Either:

- Add these named patches to `blockMeshDict`'s `boundary` section, **OR**
- Update the boundary condition files to use the actual patch names present in `blockMeshDict`: `inlet`, `outlet`, and `walls`

For example, in the `T` file, replace references to `innerWall`, `outerWall`, `lowerWall`, `upperWall` with `walls` (using `zeroGradient`), while keeping `inlet` and `outlet` as they are.

---

## Summary of Actions

1. **Fix `blockMeshDict`**: Correct the vertex ordering for all hex blocks to eliminate "inside-out" errors. Simplify the block structure if needed.
2. **Align patch names**: Ensure all boundary condition files use patch names that exist in `blockMeshDict`.
3. **Re-run `blockMesh`** after fixing the mesh definition, then run `rhoSimpleFoam`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire file with a corrected blockMeshDict that defines a proper 3D square bend mesh; Define vertices for a simple L-shaped duct: inlet arm along x-axis from x=-50 to x=0 at y=±25, bend section turning in the xy-plane, and outlet arm extending from the bend in the -y direction; Use 4 vertices per block with proper right-hand-rule ordering; Create 3 blocks: inlet straight section, bend section, outlet straight section; Fix Block 0 hex definition to proper vertex ordering like hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) or equivalent depending on vertex positions; Fix Block 1 similarly; Remove problematic extra blocks referencing non-existent vertices; Define boundary patches as inlet, outlet, and walls with correct face definitions matching the new block topology; Remove mergePatchPairs section'), PlannedFileChange(file='0/T', changes='Keep inlet fixedValue 1500; Keep outlet inletOutlet with inletValue 300; Replace innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall entries with a single walls entry using type zeroGradient'), PlannedFileChange(file='0/U', changes='Replace innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall entries with a single walls entry using type noSlip; Keep inlet and outlet entries unchanged'), PlannedFileChange(file='0/p', changes='Replace innerWall, outerWall, lowerWall, upperWall entries with a single walls entry using type zeroGradient')]
